steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Convert a speed of $\tfrac{6}{25}$ feet per second to yards per second, using the fact that $3$ feet $= 1$ yard.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
Dividing feet by $3$ converts to yards: $\dfrac{6}{25} \div 3 = \dfrac{6}{75} = \dfrac{2}{25}$.
  • Choice A — 25: multiplied instead of converted units. ✗
  • Choice B — 9: added the conversion factor instead of dividing. ✗
  • Choice C — $\frac{18}{25}$: multiplied by $3$ instead of dividing by $3$. ✗
  • Choice D — $\frac{2}{25}$: correctly divides by $3$ to convert feet to yards. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
